name: Carbon credit integrity screening
description: Cross-check MRV signals, public disclosures, and satellite proxies without asserting issuance or registry outcomes.
safetyLanguage: Screening and evidence review only. No automatic carbon credit issuance, registry approval, or VIU eligibility.

name: Corporate greenwashing divergence review
description: Compare voluntary disclosures to independent environmental signals and public records.
safetyLanguage: Narrative and documentary comparison — not a finding of fraud or liability.

name: Methane super-emitter monitoring
description: Column and plume-oriented screening with transport context; commercial feeds optional.
safetyLanguage: Anomaly screening requires validation; no enforcement or guilt conclusions.

name: CO₂ facility verification context
description: Satellite column context plus inventories and meteorology — not stack testing.
safetyLanguage: Column CO₂ cannot alone verify facility emissions without modeling and independent measurements.

name: Harmful algal bloom watch
description: Ocean color and thermal context for bloom risk triage in coastal and lake settings.
safetyLanguage: Remote sensing suggests conditions; field assays confirm toxicity and species.

name: Plastic pollution watch
description: Coastal/ocean color proxies and vessel activity context — no polymer identification claim from color alone.
safetyLanguage: Use “suggestive” language; high-resolution or field validation required for debris attribution.

name: Blue carbon MRV context
description: Combine wetland extent, structure samples, and tide context for screening-level MRV inputs.
safetyLanguage: Not certified carbon stock; no automatic credit issuance.

name: Forest carbon / deforestation audit
description: Optical/SAR change detection with biomass samples where available.
safetyLanguage: Disturbance screening is not legal land tenure or enforcement.

name: Illegal mining / land disturbance screening
description: SAR/optical disturbance flags with public records — intent and legality require investigation.
safetyLanguage: Use “disturbance detected”; no guilt, permit violation, or enforcement conclusions.

name: Water pollution evidence packet
description: Combine optical water indices, public discharge records, and lab/field validation hooks.
safetyLanguage: Screening and record linkage — adjudication requires qualified experts.

name: Flood risk and damage intelligence
description: Hydrology context: SAR wetness, gauges, SWOT, weather windows — not an emergency dispatch system.
safetyLanguage: Does not replace official warnings or legal flood determinations.

name: Wildfire / illegal burn monitoring
description: Hotspot timelines with SAR/optical corroboration — arson or illegality not inferred automatically.
safetyLanguage: Hotspots are not proof of cause, intent, or jurisdiction-specific illegality.

name: Urban heat / environmental justice context
description: Thermal and land-cover screening with demographic overlays added downstream in workflows.
safetyLanguage: Exposure screening is not clinical outcome proof.

name: Industrial air pollution watch
description: NO₂ columns, TEMPO diurnal context, and facility registry linkage.
safetyLanguage: Column abundances are not ground concentrations or permit violations alone.

name: Port and shipping pollution screening
description: Vessel activity, NO₂, ocean color turbidity proxies near ports.
safetyLanguage: AIS gaps and atmospheric mixing limit attribution.

name: Agriculture carbon / water efficiency screening
description: Soil moisture, ET/thermal stress, and optical crop condition proxies.
safetyLanguage: Agronomic decisions require local agronomy validation.

name: Drought and groundwater stress screening
description: GRACE large-basin trends with moisture and weather context — not well-specific adjudication.
safetyLanguage: GRACE cannot localize to individual wells; legal groundwater claims need dedicated studies.

name: Biodiversity / habitat integrity screening
description: Land-cover dynamics and structure samples — species richness needs ecological field survey.
safetyLanguage: Remote indices are not species inventories or regulatory biodiversity sign-offs.

name: Infrastructure subsidence monitoring
description: InSAR-capable sources and structure height references where available.
safetyLanguage: Commercial SAR and future missions are gated; deformation screening requires expert QA.

name: Dam, reservoir, and river integrity context
description: Stage/discharge, SWOT, and SAR wetness for hydrologic anomaly triage.
safetyLanguage: Not a dam safety certification or regulatory sign-off.

name: Insurance environmental risk context
description: Multi-hazard screening stack: flood, fire, heat, air, and subsidence leads.
safetyLanguage: Not underwriting, pricing, or legal advice.

name: Real estate / land due diligence
description: Historical land change, subsidence hints, and environmental record proximity.
safetyLanguage: Environmental due diligence requires qualified professionals and local records review.

name: Hazardous waste integrity audit support
description: RCRA/ECHO-style registry context with field and lab validation hooks.
safetyLanguage: Registry presence is not proof of current noncompliance; no enforcement conclusions.

name: Supplier environmental watch
description: Disclosure + facility signals + optional commercial imagery.
safetyLanguage: Supplier screening is not contractual certification.

name: Government transparency dashboard
description: Aggregate public datasets and satellite context for civic intelligence dashboards.
safetyLanguage: Dashboards surface records; they do not judge officials or establish liability.

name: Community environmental reporting
description: Triage community reports with corroborating sensors and imagery.
safetyLanguage: Community reports are pending verification until corroborated.

name: Environmental litigation support (evidence organization)
description: Structured evidence lanes for counsel workflows — DPAL does not provide legal advice.
safetyLanguage: Organizational tool only; counsel determines admissibility and strategy.

name: Investor climate risk intelligence
description: Combine disclosures and physical hazard screening without security offering claims.
safetyLanguage: Not investment advice; no forward performance or compliance guarantees.

name: Municipal climate resilience screening
description: Heat, flood, drought, and air screening layers for planning context.
safetyLanguage: Planning support only; engineering designs require licensed professionals.

name: DPAL verified impact units (governance-gated)
description: Placeholder lane for future governance products — no automatic VIU issuance.
safetyLanguage: No automatic VIU eligibility, credits, or blockchain anchoring.
